factorfiction.statement: About 71% of Earth's surface is covered by water.
factorfiction.explanation: Correct! Earth's oceans cover about 71% of the surface.

factorfiction.statement: About 71% of Earth's surface is covered by land.
factorfiction.explanation: Not quite — it is 71% water, not land.

factorfiction.statement: Earth has a magnetic field generated by its molten core.
factorfiction.explanation: Correct! The molten iron core generates Earth's protective magnetic field.

factorfiction.statement: Earth's magnetic field is generated by its atmosphere.
factorfiction.explanation: The magnetic field comes from the molten core, not the atmosphere.

factorfiction.statement: Humans first landed on the Moon in 1969.
factorfiction.explanation: Correct! Apollo 11 landed on the Moon on 20 July 1969.

factorfiction.statement: Humans first landed on the Moon in 1985.
factorfiction.explanation: The Moon landing was in 1969, not 1985.

factorfiction.statement: Earth is the only known planet that supports life.
factorfiction.explanation: Correct! Earth remains the only confirmed life-bearing planet we know of.

factorfiction.statement: Mars is the only known planet that supports life.
factorfiction.explanation: Only Earth is confirmed to support life — Mars has none confirmed yet.

factorfiction.statement: Earth's Moon stabilises the tilt of our planet.
factorfiction.explanation: Correct! Without the Moon, Earth's axial tilt would vary chaotically.

factorfiction.statement: Earth's Moon causes Earth to spin faster over time.
factorfiction.explanation: The Moon actually slows Earth's rotation slightly through tidal forces.

matchtheplanet.clue: This planet produces energy through nuclear fusion, converting hydrogen into helium every second.
Even though this body is closest to the Sun, sunlight still takes about eight minutes to reach Earth from it.
This object contains about 99.8% of all the mass in our solar system.
This planet travels around the Sun faster than any other, completing a full orbit in just 88 Earth days.
Without an atmosphere to trap heat, this planet swings from 430 degrees in the day to minus 180 at night.
Scientists have found water ice hidden inside deep craters near the poles of this planet.
This planet is the hottest in the solar system because its thick atmosphere traps heat like a greenhouse.
On this planet the Sun appears to rise in the west and set in the east.
The clouds surrounding this planet are made mostly of sulfuric acid.
About 71% of this planet's surface is covered by water.
This planet has a strong magnetic shield generated by its molten core that protects it from solar radiation.
Humans first landed on the moon of this planet in 1969.
This planet gets its reddish appearance from iron oxide dust covering much of its surface.
This planet is home to Olympus Mons, the largest volcano in the entire solar system.
Robotic missions from Earth are currently exploring this planet searching for signs of ancient water.
This planet has a gigantic storm called the Great Red Spot that has been raging for more than 300 years.
This planet has more than 95 known moons, some of which may contain hidden oceans beneath their icy surfaces.
More than 1,300 Earth-sized planets could fit inside this planet if it were hollow.
This planet's rings are made of countless chunks of ice and rock ranging from tiny grains to mountain-sized pieces.
This planet is the least dense in the solar system — it would float if placed in a gigantic ocean.
One of this planet's moons, Titan, has lakes and rivers made of liquid methane.
This planet rotates almost completely on its side with an axial tilt of about 98 degrees.
This planet has the coldest atmosphere of any planet, reaching about minus 224 degrees Celsius.
This planet has a system of rings made from dark particles and debris, though they are not as bright as its neighbour's.
This planet was predicted using mathematics before it was ever seen through a telescope.
This planet has the strongest winds in the solar system, reaching speeds of more than 2,000 kilometres per hour.
Despite its icy name, this planet's interior may contain a hot slushy ocean of water, methane and ammonia.
